issue.title: Dependency graph: click a node to open a live success-criteria panel

successCriteria.description: Clicking any node opens a panel listing that issue's success criteria with live checkbox (verified/pending) state and an N/M verified count.
The checklist ticks live as a phase completes — no manual refresh, no reselection — riding #256's planChanged -> planNonce -> plan re-read path.
A node with no plan or no success criteria shows a graceful empty state, not a crash.
The panel overlays the graph canvas without disturbing the toolbar/legend, closes cleanly, and is dismissed when the selected release changes.
No React 19 useSyncExternalStore render-loop regression (no inline ?? {} / ?? [] in new selectors; existing freshRef guard tests stay green; tsc -b clean; desktop vitest stays green).

phases.title: Expose per-issue plan data (with phase numbers) from the graph hook
phases.content:
Make the success-criteria + coverage + per-phase-with-number data available to the graph view. #256 already retains successCriteria and coverageMatrix on FetchedIssue, but it retains phases as { status }[] WITHOUT the phase number — and deriveCriteriaChecklist needs phase.number to resolve coverageMatrix entries. Fix that and surface a lookup.

In apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/useDependencyGraph.ts:

1. Widen the retained phase shape from { status: string } to { number: number; status: string } in THREE places: the PlanShape.phases type, the FetchedIssue.phases type, AND both retain sites — the main fetch effect (currently `phases.map((p) => ({ status: p.status }))`, ~line 135) and the planNonce live-refresh effect (~line 209). Map to `({ number: p.number, status: p.status })`. NOTE: derivePhaseProgressFromPlan (#256) takes PhaseLike { status } and is structurally compatible — adding `number` does not break it; confirm phase progress still works.

2. Build a `planByIssue` lookup in the existing node-builder useMemo (the one that maps fetchedIssues -> nodes, deps [release, fetchedIssues, activeWork, recentIssues]): `Record<number, { successCriteria?: {...}; coverageMatrix?: Record<string, number[]>; phases?: { number: number; status: string }[] }>` keyed by issue.number, populated from each fetched issue's retained successCriteria / coverageMatrix / phases. Building it in this memo means it re-derives whenever fetchedIssues changes — including the #256 planNonce patch — so the panel stays live.

3. Return `planByIssue` from the hook alongside { nodes, edges, isLoading, error, hasEdges, issueCount }.

Keep all changes in this one file. No new Zustand selectors here.
verification: PlanShape.phases and FetchedIssue.phases are { number: number; status: string }[]; both retain sites map number + status
useDependencyGraph returns planByIssue keyed by issue number, populated from retained plan fields
#256 phase progress still works: derivePhaseProgressFromPlan unaffected; desktop vitest stays green (196)
phases.summary: useDependencyGraph.ts: widened retained phase shape to {number,status} in 4 spots (PlanShape, FetchedIssue, main fetch effect, planNonce refresh effect); built+returned planByIssue lookup (Record<number,{successCriteria?,coverageMatrix?,phases?}>) from the node-builder memo (empty {} default in both paths). #256 phase progress unaffected. tsc -b clean; vitest 196.

phases.title: Extract reusable CriteriaChecklistView from SuccessCriteriaChecklist
phases.content:
Make the success-criteria checklist render reusable by the graph panel WITHOUT fabricating a heavy EditorPlan and without changing the detail panel's behavior.

In apps/desktop/src/components/detail/SuccessCriteriaChecklist.tsx:
- Extract the presentational render — the `<details className="criteria-checklist">` block (header with 'Success criteria' + 'N/M verified' count, and the `<ul>` of rows) — into a new EXPORTED component `CriteriaChecklistView({ rows }: { rows: CriterionVerification[] })`. It returns null when rows.length === 0, computes verifiedCount = rows.filter(r => r.verified).length internally, and renders the existing markup and CSS classes verbatim (criteria-checklist, __header, __count, __list, __item, __check, __id, __desc, __phases).
- Keep `SuccessCriteriaChecklist({ plan })` as a thin wrapper: keep its existing useMemo that derives rows via deriveCriteriaChecklist(...) from the EditorPlan, then render `<CriteriaChecklistView rows={rows} />`. Net markup + behavior for the detail panel is IDENTICAL.
- Reuse the existing `import './SuccessCriteriaChecklist.css'` (shared by both). CriterionVerification type already lives in utils/criteriaVerification — import the type as today.

This is a pure refactor: no behavior change. The detail-panel checklist must look and behave exactly as before.
verification: CriteriaChecklistView is exported from SuccessCriteriaChecklist.tsx and takes { rows: CriterionVerification[] }
SuccessCriteriaChecklist still renders identically (wraps CriteriaChecklistView); detail panel checklist unchanged
tsc -b clean; desktop vitest stays green
phases.summary: SuccessCriteriaChecklist.tsx: extracted exported CriteriaChecklistView({rows}) (null on empty, computes verifiedCount, renders the existing <details> markup verbatim); SuccessCriteriaChecklist now derives rows then returns <CriteriaChecklistView>. Detail panel byte-identical. tsc -b clean; vitest 196.

phases.title: GraphNodePanel component + styles
phases.content:
Create the click-to-open side panel that renders a live success-criteria checklist for one issue, fed entirely by props (no store access -> no fresh-ref risk).

Create apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/GraphNodePanel.tsx:
- Props: `{ issueNumber: number; title: string; plan?: { successCriteria?: { id: string; description: string; category?: string }[]; coverageMatrix?: Record<string, number[]>; phases?: { number: number; status: string }[] }; onClose: () => void }`.
- Memoize rows: build a ChecklistPlanLike `{ successCriteria: (plan?.successCriteria ?? []).map(c => ({ id: c.id, description: c.description })), phases: (plan?.phases ?? []).map(p => ({ number: p.number, status: p.status })), coverageMatrix: plan?.coverageMatrix ?? {} }` and pass to deriveCriteriaChecklist(...) from utils/criteriaVerification. Memo dep: [plan].
- Render an overlay panel: a header row with `#{issueNumber}` + a truncated title and a close button (aria-label 'Close', calls onClose); body renders `<CriteriaChecklistView rows={rows} />` (imported from ../detail/SuccessCriteriaChecklist, Phase 2). When rows.length === 0 (no plan or no criteria), render a graceful empty state ('No success criteria for this issue.') instead — never throw.
- No useTikiStateStore / Zustand selectors inside this component (data arrives via props). Liveness comes from the parent re-rendering with a fresh `plan` prop.

Create apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/GraphNodePanel.css:
- `.graph-node-panel`: position absolute; top 0; right 0; bottom 0; width ~300px; z-index 10 (must sit above the legend/banner at z-index 5 in DependencyGraph.css); background var(--bg-secondary); border-left 1px var(--border-primary); overflow-y auto; padding. Header row flex space-between; close button minimal. Include `[data-theme="light"]` parity rules consistent with DependencyGraph.css / IssueNode.css.

No render test (project uses the `node` vitest environment with no DOM/testing-library — see vitest.config.ts; criteria-tick logic is already covered by criteriaVerification.test.ts). Verify by build + the empty-state branch being explicit.
verification: GraphNodePanel.tsx renders a header (#number + title + close button) and, for a plan with criteria, a CriteriaChecklistView with live verified/pending rows + N/M count
plan undefined OR zero criteria -> graceful empty-state message, no throw
No Zustand selectors in the component (props-only) -> no fresh-ref risk
GraphNodePanel.css overlays right side at z-index 10 (above legend z-index 5); light-theme parity present
tsc -b clean; desktop vitest stays green
phases.summary: Created GraphNodePanel.tsx (props-only: issueNumber/title/plan?/onClose; builds ChecklistPlanLike -> deriveCriteriaChecklist memo on [plan]; renders header #num+title+close button and CriteriaChecklistView, or a graceful "No success criteria for this issue." empty state when rows are empty) + GraphNodePanel.css (right-side absolute overlay, width 300px, z-index 10 above the legend z-index 5, scrollable body, light-theme parity). No Zustand selectors -> no fresh-ref risk. tsc -b clean; vitest 196.

phases.title: Wire click-to-open into DependencyGraph
phases.content:
Connect node clicks to the panel.

In apps/desktop/src/components/dependencies/DependencyGraph.tsx (DependencyGraphInner):
- Consume `planByIssue` from useDependencyGraph (now returned by Phase 1): `const { nodes, edges, isLoading, error, hasEdges, issueCount, planByIssue } = useDependencyGraph(selectedVersion, releases);`.
- Add local state: `const [selectedNodeId, setSelectedNodeId] = useState<number | null>(null);`.
- Add `onNodeClick={(_evt, node) => setSelectedNodeId(parseInt(node.id, 10))}` to the <ReactFlow> element (node ids are strings; get_plan/lookup want a number). Keep the existing onNodeMouseEnter/Leave handlers.
- Render <GraphNodePanel> INSIDE `.dependency-graph-container` when selectedNodeId !== null: pass issueNumber={selectedNodeId}; title from the node data `nodes.find(n => Number(n.id) === selectedNodeId)?.data.title ?? ''`; plan={planByIssue[selectedNodeId]}; onClose={() => setSelectedNodeId(null)}.
- Dismiss on release switch: add a useEffect `useEffect(() => { setSelectedNodeId(null); }, [selectedVersion])` so a stale issue from another release never lingers.
- Do NOT route through detailStore.setSelectedIssue — this panel is local to the graph view.
- Liveness: planByIssue re-derives when fetchedIssues changes (the #256 planNonce patch), so DependencyGraph re-renders and passes a fresh `plan` to the panel -> checklist ticks live.

Keep selectedNodeId as plain local state; no new Zustand selectors.
verification: Clicking a node opens GraphNodePanel for that issue; clicking the close button clears it; switching the release dropdown dismisses the panel
Panel overlays the canvas without disturbing the toolbar/legend (renders inside .dependency-graph-container)
Live: a plan/phase status change ticks the open panel's checklist without reselection (rides #256 planChanged)
No detailStore coupling; no render-loop (existing freshRef guard tests stay green)
tsc -b clean; desktop vitest stays green; manual: open Dependency Graph, click a node, verify criteria + live tick
phases.summary: DependencyGraph.tsx: imported GraphNodePanel; consume planByIssue from useDependencyGraph; added local selectedNodeId state + onNodeClick (parseInt node.id) on <ReactFlow>; render <GraphNodePanel> inside .dependency-graph-container (title from nodes.find by id, plan from planByIssue[selectedNodeId], onClose clears); useEffect clears selectedNodeId on selectedVersion change. No detailStore coupling; no new Zustand selectors. tsc -b clean; vitest 196.
